Step 7b
⍞ Materials: Fifty Steps in Sight Singing

➜ RECAP
Review concepts from the last lesson.

➜ WARM UP
Start low and sing from "do" -> "do," then up another octave, then all the way back down to the first "do."
∞ Video Link: Warm-Ups
∞ Audio Link: Warm-Ups

➜ LISTEN & PRACTICE
Pause the audio as needed to go over the sol-fa; use hand signs. Note that the small numbers on some notes' lower right indicate the note is below “do."
∞ Audio Link: Step 7b
p.4

Step 7c
⍞ Materials: Fifty Steps in Sight Singing

➜ RECAP
Review concepts from the last lesson.

➜ WARM UP
Prepare your voice for singing.
∞ Video Link: Warm-Ups
∞ Audio Link: Warm-Ups

➜ LISTEN & PRACTICE
The little "1" next to "do" (upper right) means it is the high "do." The "1" next to "ti" (bottom right) is the low "ti," but for this lesson, cross out that "1" as we are singing the normal "ti." Try singing the exercise by yourself after listening.
∞ Audio Link: Step 7c

➜ RECAP
Review concepts from the last lesson.

∞ Video Link: Warm-Ups
∞ Audio Link: Warm-Ups

➜ LISTEN & PRACTICE
Follow along in the book on p.7 and sing as prompted or after the teacher. If your student needs the sol-fa in order to sing the second exercise in Step 10, the sol-fa syllables are:
“d, m, r, d, s, s, l, t, d, s, m, f, s, l, t, d, s, f, m, r, d.”
